summary: Renamed several fighter source, header, and asm files, primarily to make PR #717 easier to merge and avoid git confusion around overlapping Yoshi file renames. The main build-order updates were in obj_files.mk, with ftyoshi becoming ftyoshi1, the previous ftyoshi1 becoming ftyoshi2, related asm renamed to ftyoshi4, and analogous numbered cleanup for Koopa, Seak, and Master Hand files.
what_changed: Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES references the renamed fighter source and asm object paths.
Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftyoshi.c to ftyoshi1.c and removed the old ftyoshi.c path from the build list.
Renamed the previous Yoshi numbered file so ftyoshi1 became ftyoshi2, with corresponding header updates.
Renamed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftyoshi.s to asm/melee/ft/chara/ftyoshi4.s at 100% similarity.
Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftKoopa/ftkoopa.c to ftkoopa1.c and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.s to ftkoopa3.s.
Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftseak.c to ftseak1.c and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftseak.s to ftseak3.s.
Renamed Master Hand files ftMasterHand_1 through ftMasterHand_9 to zero-padded ftMasterHand_01 through ftMasterHand_09.
Updated self-includes and cross-includes in renamed Master Hand files, for example ftMasterHand_09.c now includes ftMasterHand_09.h and ftMasterHand_08.h.
Touched src/melee/ft/fighter.c for a single include/path reference update related to the renames.
smart_moves: Kept most renamed files at 100% similarity, minimizing behavioral risk while allowing git to track rename intent.
Separated a mechanical rename PR from the larger follow-up work in #717, reducing merge conflict complexity.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the file renames, preserving build ordering and object path correctness.
Used zero-padded Master Hand file numbers from _01 through _09, which sort consistently alongside _10, _11, and _12.
Adjusted include directives immediately after renames, preventing stale header references from surviving the mechanical move.
decomp_lessons: For decomp repositories with many numbered partial files, file renames can be worth isolating into a dedicated PR before merging larger source changes.
When moving a file from asm to src naming families or changing numbered segments, update obj_files.mk at the same time so the link order remains explicit.
Zero-padding numeric file suffixes avoids awkward lexicographic ordering where _10 sorts near _1 instead of after _09.
Git can mis-detect chained renames such as ftyoshi to ftyoshi1 and ftyoshi1 to ftyoshi2; make the intended mapping explicit and keep content changes minimal.
Header names and include paths should mirror renamed C files exactly to keep future search and dependency cleanup straightforward.
naming_conventions: Yoshi numbered source files use lowercase ftyoshiN.c and ftyoshiN.h, e.g. ftyoshi1.c and ftyoshi2.c.
Assembly leftovers for fighter character files may use higher numbered suffixes, e.g. ftyoshi4.s, ftkoopa3.s, and ftseak3.s.
Master Hand numbered files use two-digit zero-padded suffixes for 1 through 9, e.g. ftMasterHand_01.c, ftMasterHand_08.h, and ftMasterHand_09.c.
Renamed headers keep the same basename as their corresponding source file.
obj_files.mk paths must match the exact case-sensitive fighter character directories, such as ftYoshi, ftKoopa, ftSeak, and ftMasterHand.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR was primarily mechanical renaming rather than new matching work.
Asm files were renamed with 100% similarity for ftkoopa.s to ftkoopa3.s, ftseak.s to ftseak3.s, and ftyoshi.s to ftyoshi4.s.
Keeping asm rename diffs content-identical makes it easier to distinguish file organization changes from actual decompilation or matching changes.
The PR body explicitly cites git confusion caused by chained Yoshi renames, so the tactic was to clarify rename history before merging #717.

review_feedback.comment: The available slice reports one review but no issue comments or review comments, so no detailed reviewer feedback is evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find PR #717 to see the larger change this rename PR was preparing for.
Search for remaining non-zero-padded Master Hand includes or filenames after ftMasterHand_01 through ftMasterHand_09.
Check obj_files.mk around fighter TEXT_O_FILES when adding or renaming numbered fighter files.
Search for ftyoshi.c references to ensure old Yoshi path was fully removed.
Review later Yoshi decomp PRs for reuse of the ftyoshi1 and ftyoshi2 split.
